stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - ‚úÖ **Montgomery internals + API preservation**: `Fp

` stores Montgomery form internally, while `new()`/`value()` convert at boundaries (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:105-113`, `126-131`, `145-190`; helpers in `crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/montgomery.rs:74-127`). - ‚úÖ **Axiom/correctness coverage retained**: arithmetic/inverse/trait behavior tests are present and extensive (`mod.rs:457-795`, `montgomery.rs:163-425`). - ‚úÖ **Cross-verification criterion met**: explicit proptest config uses 2000 cases and covers 5 ops for each of `Fp<3>`, `Fp<65537>`, `Fp<2^61-1>` (30,000+ random op checks total), plus exhaustive small-prime checks (`mod.rs:629-678`, `688-795`). - ‚úÖ **Benchmark criterion evidence present**: naive `%` baselines use runtime-opaque modulus via `black_box(p)` (`crates/gf2-core/benches/fp_montgomery.rs:154-159`, `165-168`, `174-177`, `183-186`, `195-197`), and committed results report thresholds met (`crates/gf2-core/benches/BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md:18-24`). - ‚úÖ **Constant-time hot path style**: add/sub/reduction use branchless correction (`montgomery.rs:79-83`, `111-127`), and mul path is REDC-based for odd primes (`mod.rs:184-189`). ## Separation of concerns - ‚úÖ Changes are confined to `gf2-core` surfaces (`src/gfp/*`, `benches/*`), and `gf2-core` has no dependency on `gf2-coding` (`crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml:12-19`). ## Functional paradigm and performance - ‚úÖ High-level API remains clean and trait-based (`mod.rs`), while low-level arithmetic uses efficient word-level operations in a dedicated helper module (`montgomery.rs`). - ‚úÖ GF(p) arithmetic in hot paths is implemented with wrapping/bitwise-friendly operations and no `%` in Montgomery add/sub/mul internals. ## Correctness - ‚úÖ Montgomery constants (`R mod P`, `R^2 mod P`, `-P^{-1} mod 2^64`) are computed as `const fn` and validated by tests (`montgomery.rs:16-23`, `30-61`, `163-216`). - ‚úÖ Core field operations are cross-checked against naive modular formulas across exhaustive and randomized coverage (`mod.rs:629-795`). - ‚úÖ BitVec tail-masking/bit-numbering invariants are unaffected (no `bitvec` mutator changes in this issue‚Äôs scope). ## Testing - ‚úÖ Test breadth includes unit tests, exhaustive checks for small primes, and proptests for target large primes (`mod.rs:629-795`, `montgomery.rs:276-425`). - ‚úÖ In provided gate context, `tests`, `clippy`, and `fmt` are marked passed (`gates_status.tests/clippy/fmt`). - ‚úÖ Test naming convention `test__` is followed throughout new coverage. ## Unsafe isolation - ‚úÖ No new unsafe code in `gf2-core` changed area; `unsafe` remains isolated to `gf2-kernels-simd` (matches in `gf2-core` source are comments/documentation only). ## Documentation - ‚úÖ Public `Fp

` docs were updated for Montgomery semantics with examples and panics context (`mod.rs:1-31`, `42-71`, `90-132`). - ‚úÖ Benchmark evidence doc exists and is directly tied to acceptance targets (`benches/BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md`). ## Prior review feedback for this issue - ‚úÖ Previously flagged gap on explicit `Fp<3>` randomized coverage is addressed (`mod.rs:688-725` with 2000-case proptest per op). - ‚úÖ Previously flagged missing benchmark evidence is addressed by committed benchmark report and naive baseline benches (`BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md`, `fp_montgomery.rs:113-201`). ## Jit issue dependencies - ‚úÖ Prerequisite `350bff7f` is marked `done` in issue context, and this work clearly builds on the existing naive `Fp

` implementation by replacing internals while preserving API. VERDICT: PASS --- Gate result: PASSED
